content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11559166/", "source_title": "Effects of different types of intermittent fasting on metabolic outcomes", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Methods We systematically searched PubMed, Embase, and the Cochrane databases for meta-analyses of randomized controlled trials (RCTs)...", "direct_quote": "Methods We systematically searched PubMed, Embase, and the Cochrane databases for meta-analyses of randomized controlled trials (RCTs) investigating the association between IF and metabolic health outcomes.", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.55, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Subsequently, we performed an umbrella review and network meta-analysis (NMA) to evaluate the efficacy of different forms of IF...", "direct_quote": "Subsequently, we performed an umbrella review and network meta-analysis (NMA) to evaluate the efficacy of different forms of IF (time-restricted eating (TRE), alternate-day fasting (ADF), and 5:2 diet (regular eating for 5 days and energy restriction", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.7000000000000001, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "To assess the certainty of both direct and indirect estimates, we employed the Confidence in Network Meta-Analysis (CINeMA) approach.", "direct_quote": "To assess the certainty of both direct and indirect estimates, we employed the Confidence in Network Meta-Analysis (CINeMA) approach.", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.55, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts from Effects of different types of intermittent fasting on metabo."}]}
